title: The fire crests Mara Ridge above Coldwater Bend
The one play: cut the saddle and walk a backfire down to meet it
Bo's cut crew opens the firebreak at the east end, working west
Wrenna lights the backfire high and starts to walk its edge down
Tam runs the line between crews, carrying the cut's progress
The saws strike a granite seam at the western draw and stall
The argument over the one water cart begins
The word to Wrenna garbles: she is told the western draw is lined
Midnight at the draw: the truth reaches Bo and the cart call is forced
Bo sends the cart down to hold the lower edge; a hand-team takes the granite
The cart holds the backfire's lower edge at the draw
The hand-team closes the granite gap; the western-draw line is shut
Held back just enough, the two fires meet at the cut and starve
The wind turns at dawn: Coldwater Bend wakes to ash on the ridge and nothing more
Bo keeps the cart with the saws to beat the granite faster
Dry on the lower edge, the backfire jumps the open draw and crosses the line
The crossed fire runs downslope; the saws never beat the granite in time
The wind turns at dawn and drives the loosed fire down onto the town
